title: Best VPN for Remote Work 2026: Secure Your Connection From Anywhere
description: Remote workers need a VPN that balances security, speed, and reliability. These are the best VPNs for working from home or on the road in 2026.
content: Remote work has made VPN usage mainstream, but the requirements for a remote work VPN differ from a VPN you use for streaming or basic privacy. You need consistent uptime, kill switch reliability, fast speeds that do not degrade video calls, and support for corporate network access if your employer requires it. These four picks perform best on those criteria.

NordVPN is the all-round choice for remote workers. Fast servers in 111 countries, a reliable kill switch, and NordLayer integration for teams that need managed company VPN access. The Meshnet feature lets you create a private network between devices, which is useful if you need to access your home computer remotely. NordVPN costs $3.99 per month on the two-year plan. Speeds are fast enough for 4K streaming and video calls without perceptible lag.

ExpressVPN is the choice if you travel frequently to countries with strict internet filtering, like China, the UAE, or Russia. Its Lightway protocol is specifically optimized for reliability on unstable mobile connections, which matters when you are working from a hotel or airport lounge. ExpressVPN costs more than most alternatives ($8.32 per month on a one-year plan) but its track record in restricted countries is better than any competitor. The router app is also a practical option if you want to protect an entire Airbnb or hotel room.

Mullvad VPN is the privacy-first choice. No accounts, no email required: you get a random account number when you sign up and pay by cash or Monero if you want complete anonymity. Mullvad costs 5 euros per month flat, no multi-year discount gimmicks. Speeds are competitive, and the desktop apps are cleaner than most. If your work involves sensitive client data and you want the maximum possible distance between your traffic and any identifiable information, Mullvad is the standout option.

For teams rather than individuals, Perimeter 81 (now part of Check Point) is the enterprise-grade solution. It provides a managed business VPN with centralized user management, application-layer access controls, and activity logs. It is not a consumer product, pricing starts around $8 per user per month with a minimum team size, but for companies with 10+ remote workers needing consistent access to internal resources it is worth the cost compared to patching consumer VPNs.

What to avoid: free VPNs for work purposes. Free VPN providers fund operations through data collection and advertising. On a personal device you use exclusively for entertainment, that tradeoff is your choice to make. On a device you use for work, sharing your browsing traffic with a third-party ad network is a security risk your employer would not sanction. If cost is the constraint, Mullvad at 5 euros per month is the cheapest trustworthy option.
